name: R5AttachmentElementMapToR4
title: Cross-version mapping for FHIR R5 Attachment to FHIR R4 Attachment
description: This ConceptMap represents cross-version mappings for elements from a FHIR R5 Attachment to FHIR R4.

target.comment: Element `Attachment.contentType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.contentType` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Attachment.contentType` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.contentType` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Attachment.language` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.language` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Attachment.language` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.language` as `Equivalent`.

Element `Attachment.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.data` as `Equivalent`.
The base64-encoded data SHALL be expressed in the same character set as the base resource XML or JSON.

Element `Attachment.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.data` as `Equivalent`.
The base64-encoded data SHALL be expressed in the same character set as the base resource XML or JSON.

Element `Attachment.url` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.url` as `Equivalent`.
If both data and url are provided, the url SHALL point to the same content as the data contains. Urls may be relative references or may reference transient locations such as a wrapping envelope using cid: though this has ramifications for using signatures. Relative URLs are interpreted relative to the service url, like a resource reference, rather than relative to the resource itself. If a URL is provided, it SHALL resolve to actual data.

Element `Attachment.url` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.url` as `Equivalent`.
If both data and url are provided, the url SHALL point to the same content as the data contains. Urls may be relative references or may reference transient locations such as a wrapping envelope using cid: though this has ramifications for using signatures. Relative URLs are interpreted relative to the service url, like a resource reference, rather than relative to the resource itself. If a URL is provided, it SHALL resolve to actual data.

Element `Attachment.size` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.size` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types: integer64.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.

Element `Attachment.size` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.size` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types: integer64.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.

Element `Attachment.hash` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.hash` as `Equivalent`.
The hash is calculated on the data prior to base64 encoding, if the data is based64 encoded. The hash is not intended to support digital signatures. Where protection against malicious threats a digital signature should be considered, see [Provenance.signature](https://hl7.org/fhir/provenance-definition.html#Provenance.signature) for mechanism to protect a resource with a digital signature.

Element `Attachment.hash` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.hash` as `Equivalent`.
The hash is calculated on the data prior to base64 encoding, if the data is based64 encoded. The hash is not intended to support digital signatures. Where protection against malicious threats a digital signature should be considered, see [Provenance.signature](https://hl7.org/fhir/provenance-definition.html#Provenance.signature) for mechanism to protect a resource with a digital signature.

Element `Attachment.title` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.title` as `Equivalent`.
May sometimes be derived from the source filename.

Element `Attachment.title` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.title` as `Equivalent`.
May sometimes be derived from the source filename.
Element `Attachment.creation` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.creation` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Attachment.creation` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.creation` as `Equivalent`.

Element `Attachment.size` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.size` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types: integer64.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.

Element `Attachment.height` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.height` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `Attachment.width` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.width` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `Attachment.frames` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.frames` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
if the number of frames is not supplied, the value may be unknown. Applications should not assume that there is only one frame unless it is explicitly stated.

Element `Attachment.duration` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.duration` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The duration might differ from occurrencePeriod if recording was paused.

Element `Attachment.pages` has a context of Attachment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Attachment`.
Element `Attachment.pages` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
